symbols: obelisks flanking the composition — ancient Egyptian monuments to the sun god, representing human aspiration toward eternity and divine power
winged sun disk — Egyptian symbol of divine protection and sovereignty, later echoed in biblical imagery of God's sheltering wings
sphinxes guarding the fireplace — threshold guardians between the mundane and the sacred, recalling cherubim who guard sacred spaces
hieroglyphic inscriptions — the mystery of ancient writing and lost civilizations, the record of human attempts to communicate with the divine
fire within the hearth — the primal element of warmth and transformation, recalling the burning bush and the pillar of fire
description: A detailed architectural engraving showing an elaborate fireplace design in the Egyptian Revival style. The composition is symmetrically arranged around a central fireplace opening in which flames are visible. The surround is flanked by standing Egyptian figures (caryatids) and sphinxes at the base. Above the mantel, a central standing Egyptian deity figure is framed by two tall obelisks covered in hieroglyphic inscriptions. A large winged sun disk spreads across the upper portion. Additional Egyptian motifs include scarabs, lotus capitals, and cartouches.
